response.overview: In 1995 I was teaching in San Francisco while living in an apartment close to Freeway 101  where it enters the city. Struck by the variety of life continuing regardless beneath this  huge structure, I started filming, using fixed camera positions that plot four adjacent  streets, always with the freeway in shot. The resulting single-screen film was Under the  Freeway.  Three years later I returned to San Francisco by which time the top layer of the freeway  had been removed (it was now seen as an earthquake liability). I went back to the same  locations and filmed again. The resulting two-screen film, After the Freeway, is projected  as two discrete time zones, 1995 and 1998. (Guy Sherwin)
response.title: After the Freeway

release_dates.note: Angles of Projection – symposium at Chelsea College of Art
